lesson.title: Axioms of Probability
lesson.body: Kolmogorov's three axioms define a probability measure P on a sample space Ω: (1) P(A) ≥ 0 for every event A; (2) P(Ω) = 1; (3) for any countable collection of mutually exclusive events, P(A₁ ∪ A₂ ∪ …) = ΣP(Aᵢ). From these axioms follow key theorems: P(∅) = 0, P(Aᶜ) = 1 − P(A), and the inclusion-exclusion formula P(A ∪ B) = P(A) + P(B) − P(A ∩ B).

rooms.question: Which of the following assignments is a valid probability measure on Ω = {a, b, c}?
rooms.explanation: Option A has all values ≥ 0 and sums to exactly 1 (0.5 + 0.3 + 0.2 = 1.0). Option B sums to 1.2, option C has a negative value, and option D sums to 1.2.

rooms.question: Given P(A) = 0.3, what is P(Aᶜ)?
steps: A and A^c are mutually exclusive and exhaustive: A \cup A^c = \Omega.
By axiom 2 and axiom 3: P(A) + P(A^c) = 1.
rooms.explanation: P(Aᶜ) = 1 − P(A) = 1 − 0.3 = 0.7. This follows directly from Axioms 2 and 3: A and Aᶜ partition Ω.

rooms.question: Events A and B satisfy P(A) = 0.4, P(B) = 0.5, and P(A ∩ B) = 0.2. Find P(A ∪ B).
rooms.latex: P(A \cup B) = P(A) + P(B) - P(A \cap B)
rooms.explanation: By inclusion-exclusion: P(A ∪ B) = P(A) + P(B) − P(A ∩ B) = 0.4 + 0.5 − 0.2 = 0.7. Subtracting P(A ∩ B) corrects for double-counting the overlap.

rooms.question: For mutually exclusive events A and B with P(A) = 0.25 and P(B) = 0.35, what is P(A ∪ B)?
rooms.latex: A \cap B = \emptyset \implies P(A \cup B) = P(A) + P(B)
steps: Mutual exclusivity means A \cap B = \emptyset, so P(A \cap B) = 0.
rooms.explanation: For disjoint events, P(A ∪ B) = P(A) + P(B) = 0.25 + 0.35 = 0.60. No overlap correction is needed because A ∩ B = ∅.

rooms.question: Order these probability values from smallest to largest, given that A ⊆ B ⊆ Ω and P(A)=0.2, P(B)=0.5:
rooms.explanation: For any events with A ⊆ B ⊆ Ω, monotonicity of probability gives P(∅) ≤ P(A) ≤ P(B) ≤ P(Ω). Here: 0 < 0.2 < 0.5 < 1.

boss.name: The Kolmogorov Daemon
boss.taunt: Your axioms crumble. I am the void where probability collapses to zero!

questions.question: Which Kolmogorov axiom directly implies P(∅) = 0?
answers: Axiom 3 (countable additivity): \Omega = \Omega \cup \emptyset, so P(\Omega) = P(\Omega) + P(\emptyset)
None of the axioms — it is an independent postulate
questions.explanation: Since Ω and ∅ are disjoint with Ω ∪ ∅ = Ω, Axiom 3 gives P(Ω) = P(Ω) + P(∅). Subtracting P(Ω) from both sides yields P(∅) = 0.

questions.question: Given P(A) = 0.6, P(B) = 0.5, and P(A ∪ B) = 0.8, what is P(A ∩ B)?
questions.latex: P(A \cap B) = P(A) + P(B) - P(A \cup B)
questions.explanation: Rearranging inclusion-exclusion: P(A ∩ B) = P(A) + P(B) − P(A ∪ B) = 0.6 + 0.5 − 0.8 = 0.3.

questions.question: Three mutually exclusive and exhaustive events A₁, A₂, A₃ satisfy P(A₁) = 2P(A₂) = 4P(A₃). Find P(A₁).
questions.explanation: Let P(A₃) = p. Then P(A₂) = 2p and P(A₁) = 4p. The three events partition Ω so 4p + 2p + p = 7p = 1, giving p = 1/7. Therefore P(A₁) = 4/7.
